description: Evaluates application development and testing maturity—service governance, KPIs/KRIs, secure development strategy, ownership and separation of duties, DevSecOps CI/CD, application security testing (SAST/DAST), developer production isolation, supply chain security, AI-assisted development governance, DevSecOps architecture, and expert code review.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An application development management service exists, is scoped appropriately, and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise application development management service exists
Regular application development management service efficacy review

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Application development KPIs and KRIs—including defect escape rate, pipeline security gate pass rate, and vulnerability remediation SLAs—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented KPIs and KRIs for application development
Regular KPI/KRI review
Defect escape and security gate metrics
Vulnerability remediation velocity in SDLC

capabilities.name: Secure application development strategy
capabilities.description: A documented application development strategy incorporates security early, addresses AI-era threat models, includes automation and secure provisioning, and is reviewed regularly.
evaluation_focus: Application development strategy exists
Security incorporated at early development stages
Automation of code and secure provisioning
AI-era threat model reflected in development strategy
Regular review of application development policies and strategies

capabilities.name: Application ownership and governance
capabilities.description: Clear business owners, separation of duties, business analysts, and staging-only developer access govern application development across the portfolio.
evaluation_focus: Clear business owners and separation of duties policies
Developers and testers limited to staging environments
Separation of duties for business owners and developers
Formal business analysts for requirements review and translation

capabilities.name: DevSecOps CI/CD software
capabilities.description: DevSecOps software automates development milestones, supports CI/CD for all applications, is reviewed regularly, and integrates with workload management, monitoring, compliance, and vulnerability systems.
evaluation_focus: DevSecOps software adoption
Automation support for application development milestones
CI/CD pipeline utilized for all applications
Regular DevSecOps software configuration review
Integration with workload management, monitoring, compliance, and vulnerability systems

capabilities.name: Application security testing
capabilities.description: Application security testing covers static, dynamic, and proactive analysis in CI/CD for all applications, with automation, contextual integrations, and AI-assisted pre-release checks.
evaluation_focus: Application security testing software adoption
Static and dynamic testing in CI/CD for all applications
Automated static and dynamic testing
Proactive issue identification based on playbooks
Integration with other platforms for contextual analysis
AI-assisted pre-release security analysis

capabilities.name: Developer access and production isolation
capabilities.description: CI/CD pipelines manage developer and production environment access, automatically remove developer accounts after production deployment, and detect dev account anomalies in production code.
evaluation_focus: CI/CD pipelines manage user access to developer and production environments
Developer accounts removed from all applications after production deployment
Automated developer account removal
Code scanning detects dev account anomalies in production code

capabilities.name: DevSecOps architect
capabilities.description: A formal DevSecOps architect supports secure design and standards for enterprise-wide applications.
evaluation_focus: Formal DevSecOps architect for enterprise-wide applications

capabilities.name: Expert and third-party code review
capabilities.description: External experts formally review code for all or critical applications, with emphasis on high-risk changes and AI-generated code paths.
evaluation_focus: Formal external expert code review
External code review scope for all or critical applications
Review coverage for AI-generated or AI-assisted code

capabilities.name: Software supply chain security
capabilities.description: CI/CD pipelines generate SBOMs, scan dependencies for known vulnerabilities, and enforce policies on open-source and third-party components before release.
evaluation_focus: Software bill of materials (SBOM) generation in CI/CD
Dependency and open-source vulnerability scanning in pipeline
Third-party component policy enforcement before release

capabilities.name: AI-assisted development governance
capabilities.description: Policies govern approved AI coding tools, review requirements for AI-generated code, and guardrails for AI use in development and testing workflows.
evaluation_focus: Approved versus shadow AI coding tool policy
Review and testing requirements for AI-generated code
Guardrails for AI use in development and testing workflows

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
